summary: Tight ’71 set with epic Good Lovin' and strong Around & Around climax
blurb: The band delivers a tight performance highlighted by an epic Good Lovin’ and a punchy Around & Around climax. Jerry’s guitar is loud, sweet and clearly in his element, while the setlist flows smoothly from Truckin’ to Uncle John’s Band. The soundboard captures the instruments cleanly though vocal mix quirks are noticeable.
review:
On March 20, 1971, the Grateful Dead took the Fieldhouse at the University of Iowa for a tight‑as‑a‑fiddle two‑set marathon that still feels fresh decades later. The first set rides a solid groove from Truckin’ through a rollicking Good Lovin’, with Jerry Garcia’s guitar work described as “loud, sweet and enjoyable,” clearly feeding off the energy of the crowd. Bob Weir’s vocals stumble on Turn On Your Lovelight, but the band quickly regains momentum, delivering a standout Around & Around that serves as the high‑point of an already strong second set. Pigpen’s keyboards sit comfortably in the mix, and Bill Kreutzmann anchors the rhythm with his characteristic drive. The sole available soundboard recording is instrumentally clear, letting listeners hear every nuance despite some vocal/mix oddities that can sound off‑key at times.

Overall this show stands out for its cohesive setlist flow, a particularly epic Good Lovin’, and a climactic Around & Around that showcases the group’s early‑’70s chemistry. While the vocal mix isn’t pristine, the performance quality more than makes up for it, making this recording a solid pick for anyone looking to explore the Dead’s pre‑Europe ’72 era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All available analysis is positive, praising the tight playing, epic Good Lovin’, strong Around & Around climax, and Jerry’s sweet guitar work. No negative performance comments appear across recordings, just minor vocal/mix issues, leading to a solid positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Instrumentally clear soundboard capture that showcases the band’s tight playing, despite some vocal/mix quirks.

key_highlights: Epic Good Lovin’ performance that drives the first set.
Punchy Around & Around climax in the second set.
Tight overall setlist flow with strong early‑half energy.

band_performance.Jerry: Loud, sweet and enjoyable guitar work; clearly having a great night.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal interjections on Turn On Your Lovelight are problematic, but overall contribution solid.
